articles.title: Apple's sleep apnea notifications and hearing test features are now available in India
articles.description: Apple has announced the expansion of sleep apnea notifications and hearing test features to India. Sleep apnea is a condition in which a person's breathing momentarily stops during sleep, preventing the body from getting enough oxygen. If left untreated, it can lead to health consequences over time, including increased risk of hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and cardiac issues. Apple Watch helps its users detect signs of sleep apnea. It's done with the help of an Apple Watch metric called Breathing Disturbances, which uses the smartwatch's accelerometer to detect small movements at the...

articles.title: These premium desk accessories completely upgraded my setup [Video]
articles.description: Desk setups are ever evolving. Because so much of our workflow and desk revolves around tech, which changes constantly, the products we use every day naturally change alongside it. But one thing I’ve noticed over the years is that when I invest a little more in premium accessories, they tend to last much longer. There will always be a place for budget-conscious products, but the accessories I keep coming back to are usually well-built, thoughtfully designed, and that improve my overall workflow. I wanted to share some of my favorite premium desk accessories that would benefit anyone’s setup, whether for gaming, work, or leisure. more...

articles.title: Virtual Assistants Are Getting Supercharged with AI, and Clients Are Loving It
articles.description: New Delhi [India], May 23: Spend an hour with an independent consultant in North America and a pattern shows up fast. The hours billed are not the problem. The hours not billed are. Proposals, follow-ups, decks, invoices, social media, the half-finished landing page, the tax document the accountant asked for three weeks ago. Most independent consultants do not have a revenue problem. They have an admin problem dressed up as a revenue problem.

articles.title: ‘Clock It’ goes official: Delhi Police uses viral trend to warn users online
articles.description: Delhi Police is now using popular internet slang to promote cybersecurity. The force adopted the viral phrase 'Clock It' for a new campaign. This aims to encourage social media users to be more vigilant online. The initiative highlights essential safety tips like password protection and scam awareness. This approach connects with younger audiences through familiar online trends.

articles.title: GAT-B and BET 2026 answer keys released; NTA opens challenge window till May 24
articles.description: DBT BET Answer Key 2026: The National Testing Agency has released the provisional answer keys, question papers and recorded responses for GAT-B and BET 2026 examinations conducted on May 17, 2026. Candidates can raise objections online till May 24, 2026 by paying a non-refundable fee of Rs 200 per question. The examinations were held across 54 cities for 26,662 registered candidates. NTA stated that challenges submitted by candidates will be reviewed by subject experts before preparation of the final answer key and results.

articles.title: IIT-Roorkee transfers advanced fast-charging Li-ion battery tech to industry partner
articles.description: IIT-Roorkee has transferred two advanced lithium-ion battery technologies to Cathion Energy for commercial use. Developed by Prof Anjan Sil, these novel electrode composites aim to significantly improve battery charging speed and power density. This breakthrough could reduce charging times for electric vehicles and electronics from an hour to under 10 minutes.

articles.title: Amazon founder to all those predicting AI bubble: Even if it turns out to be a bubble, you should not worry because ...
articles.description: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos dismissed fears of an AI bubble, stating that even if current valuations are unsustainable, the long-term benefits will outweigh the risks. He likened the AI boom to the 1990s biotech bubble, where initial losses led to significant advancements.

articles.title: Google users were left stunned after searching this one word on May 22
articles.description: Google Search users were surprised this week when typing 'disregard' yielded a chatbot-like AI response instead of definitions. The peculiar glitch, which also affected searches for 'forget' and 'disappointed' with oddly comforting replies, sparked online amusement and renewed criticism of Google's AI integration. The issue, however, was reportedly fixed shortly after.

articles.title: Xiaomi 17 Max gets disassembled on video
articles.description: Yesterday, Xiaomi announced the 17 Max, and today there's already a full teardown / disassembly video on YouTube showing all of its insides, courtesy of the Chinese channel WekiHome. While the video is in Chinese, we find YouTube's English language captions very good, so make sure you turn them on if you don't speak Chinese. The Xiaomi 17 Max is a rather surprising arrival, since it's the first Max in the company's flagship line. The Xiaomi 17, Xiaomi 17 Pro, and Xiaomi 17 Pro Max all came out in China in September, followed by the Xiaomi 17 Ultra in December. Thus, we're five months...

articles.title: Who killed US President Donald Trump's AI order? Musk says it wasn't him
articles.description: President Donald Trump's executive order on powerful AI models has collapsed. Allies in Silicon Valley reportedly convinced the president to pull the plug. The order aimed to implement new AI cybersecurity measures. This development highlights Washington's struggle to agree on AI guardrails. The United States lags behind Europe and Asia in AI regulation.
